changed_functions.name: AfdTLListenComplete (sub_1C005AB60)
key_changes: New call to AfdDrainListeningIrps (0x1C001687C) added before the spinlock release on the failed-listen (status < 0) branch, gated by EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2949905720_61057653)

AfdDrainListeningIrps walks the pending listen-IRP list at conn+0x70 (each list entry lies at IRP+0xA8, so IRP = entry - 0xA8), clears each IRP's cancel routine, completes it with the failing status, and re-acquires the conn+0x30 spinlock
Only the failed-listen branch is affected; the success branch is unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the listen completion handler. The abort branch runs only when the completion status a2 < 0. In the unpatched version it clears state flags ([conn+8] &= ~3, conn[0xA8], conn[0xAA], *conn &= ~4) and releases the conn+0x30 spinlock WITHOUT draining the pending listen-IRP list at conn+0x70; AfdDerefTLBaseEndpoint then runs. Any IRPs still queued on conn+0x70 therefore survive across the endpoint dereference and can later reference a freed endpoint. The patched version, when the feature flag is enabled, calls AfdDrainListeningIrps(conn, &LockHandle, status) to drain and complete those IRPs before the lock is released.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED (failed-listen branch, no drain):
and     dword ptr [rbx+8], 0FFFFFFFCh
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+90h]
mov     [rbx+0A8h], ax
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+78h]
mov     [rbx+0AAh], ax
mov     eax, 0FFFBh
and     [rbx], ax
call    cs:__imp_KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

; PATCHED (drain added before release):
and     dword ptr [rbx+8], 0FFFFFFFCh
lea     rcx, g_Feature_2949905720_61057653_FeatureDescriptorDetails
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+90h]
mov     [rbx+0A8h], ax
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+78h]
mov     [rbx+0AAh], ax
mov     eax, 0FFFBh
and     [rbx], ax
call    EvaluateCurrentState
test    eax, eax
je      short loc_1C005995F
mov     r8d, ebp               ; status
lea     rdx, [rsp+88h+LockHandle]
mov     rcx, rbx               ; connection
call    AfdDrainListeningIrps  ; NEW: drain conn+0x70 list
call    cs:__imp_KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

changed_functions.name: AfdSanFastCompleteRequest (sub_1C0078630)
key_changes: The teardown-state check cmp byte [r15+0x91], 0 is made unconditional in the patched version
Removed the EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_757798200_60363861) call that previously gated whether [r15+0x91] was checked
Cancel routine is AfdSanCancelRequest in BOTH builds (the earlier claim of two different cancel-routine addresses was an address-rebasing artifact)
Whole sequence (cancel-routine set, Cancel check, state check, list insertion onto conn+0x70) runs under the conn+0x30 spinlock
changed_functions.security_notes: This is an AFD receive completion path. The original TOCTOU characterization is NOT supported by the binaries: the cancel-routine set, the Irp->Cancel check, the connection-state check, and the list insertion all execute while the conn+0x30 spinlock is held, and AfdSanCancelRequest also acquires that lock, so no concurrent cancel can interleave. The actual change is narrower and defensive: the unpatched queue condition is Irp->Cancel(Irp+0x44)==0 && (!EvaluateCurrentState(flag) || conn[0x91]==0), so when the feature flag is disabled the teardown-state byte conn[0x91] is not checked and the receive IRP is queued onto conn+0x70 regardless. The patched condition is Irp->Cancel==0 && conn[0x91]==0, making the teardown-state check unconditional. This is a hardening improvement, not a race; severity is low.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED (state check gated by feature flag):
lea     rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
xchg    rax, [rsi+68h]              ; Irp->CancelRoutine
cmp     byte ptr [rsi+44h], 0      ; Irp->Cancel
jne     short loc_1C00789D2
lea     rcx, g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails
call    EvaluateCurrentState
test    eax, eax
je      short loc_1C0078995        ; flag off -> SKIP [r15+91h] check, queue IRP
cmp     byte ptr [r15+91h], 0
jne     short loc_1C00789D2
; queue IRP onto conn+0x70 list

; PATCHED (state check unconditional):
lea     rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
xchg    rax, [rsi+68h]
cmp     byte ptr [rsi+44h], 0
jne     short loc_1C0077A92
cmp     byte ptr [r15+91h], 0      ; always checked
jne     short loc_1C0077A92
; queue IRP onto conn+0x70 list

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00529A0
key_changes: Restructured connection cleanup/abort path with added feature-flag-gated state checks around the drain logic
changed_functions.security_notes: Connection abort/cleanup function with restructured drain logic and added feature-gated state checks. Listed as a security-relevant candidate on the basis of the change; the exact mechanism was not further verified in this pass, so no specific offsets or callee names are asserted.

changed_functions.name: AfdSanAcceptCore (sub_1C007608C)
key_changes: In the options-copy block (guarded by options_size >= 6), the branch taken when connection byte [conn+0xAC] == 0 computes the copy length as options_size - 8 in a 16-bit register
Patched version adds EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_1191968056_61054031); when enabled, it checks options_size < 8 and zero-fills with memset instead of subtracting 8
The other branch ([conn+0xAC] != 0) subtracts 2 and is unchanged; it cannot underflow because the guard already forces options_size >= 6
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the accept IRP completion routine that copies connection-response options data into the user's MDL-mapped buffer. In the subtract-8 branch, options_size - 8 is a 16-bit operation; because the only guard is options_size >= 6, the values 6 and 7 pass and wrap to 0xFFFE/0xFFFF, driving an oversized memmove (up to ~65534 bytes) into the mapped output buffer. The overflow is bounded to a 16-bit length (<= ~64 KB), not an unbounded copy. The patched version, when the feature flag is enabled, checks options_size < 8 and zero-fills with memset instead of subtracting 8.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED (subtract-8 branch, [conn+0xAC]==0):
movzx   eax, r14w                   ; options size
sub     eax, 8                      ; size - 8
cmp     ecx, eax
jge     short loc_1C00764BD
lea     r14d, [r9+r8]
jmp     short loc_1C00764C6
loc_1C00764BD:
mov     eax, 0FFF8h
add     r14w, ax                    ; 16-bit size - 8 -> 0FFFEh/0FFFFh for size 6/7
loc_1C00764C6:
movzx   r8d, r14w                   ; Size (underflowed)
lea     rcx, [r15+8]
call    memmove                     ; OVERSIZED copy

; PATCHED (feature-gated < 8 zero-fill):
lea     rcx, g_Feature_1191968056_61054031_FeatureDescriptorDetails
call    EvaluateCurrentState
mov     ebx, 8
test    eax, eax
jz      short loc_1C0075512         ; feature off -> old subtract-8 path
cmp     si, bx                      ; options size < 8 ?
jb      short loc_1C0075502         ; yes -> zero-fill
sub     si, bx                      ; size - 8 (safe, size >= 8)
loc_1C0075502:
movzx   r8d, si
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, r15
call    memset                      ; SAFE

Connection state condition split into separate branches for afd1/afd1afd types with different flag checking
When feature flag is on, checks (*(arg1+5)&0x10) and arg1[3]&1 separately instead of as combined condition

security_findings.entry_point: AFD accept IRP completion — reached via NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd during accept/super-accept handling
call_chain: AfdSanAcceptCore (accept IRP completion routine — copies options data from the connection response into the user's MDL-mapped buffer)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Integer underflow leading to heap buffer overflow (kernel pool)
security_findings.description: In AfdSanAcceptCore (unpatched 0x1C007608C, patched 0x1C007508C), the options-copy block runs only when options_size (word at [connection+0x18]) >= 6. Inside it, when the connection byte at [connection+0xAC] == 0, the copy length is computed as options_size - 8 in a 16-bit register. Because the only guard is options_size >= 6, the values 6 and 7 pass the guard and options_size - 8 wraps in 16 bits to 0xFFFE (65534) or 0xFFFF (65535). That wrapped value is used as the memmove size, copying up to ~64 KB from the connection-response options data into the MDL-mapped output buffer that was sized for the small options field. The overflow is bounded to a 16-bit length (<= ~64 KB), not an unbounded copy. The other branch ([connection+0xAC] != 0) subtracts 2 and is unchanged between builds; it cannot underflow because the guard already forces options_size >= 6. The patched version adds EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_1191968056_61054031) and, when enabled, checks options_size < 8 and zero-fills with memset instead of subtracting 8.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Create a listening AFD socket (SOCK_STREAM, TCP) and post an accept/super-accept operation (AcceptEx / WSAAccept), reaching AfdSanAcceptCore via AfdAccept / AfdServiceSuperAccept / AfdServiceWaitForListen. 2. The completion must reach the options block: options_size at [connection+0x18] >= 6 and the connection byte at [connection+0xAC] == 0 (so the subtract-8 branch runs). 3. Drive options_size to exactly 6 or 7 so options_size - 8 wraps in 16 bits to 0xFFFE/0xFFFF. 4. The memmove then copies ~65534/65535 bytes into the MDL-mapped output buffer.
security_findings.primitive: Bounded kernel buffer overflow. memmove copies up to a 16-bit length (max 0xFFFF, <= ~64 KB) from the connection-response options data into the MDL-mapped output buffer, overrunning the buffer's mapped extent into adjacent kernel memory. Standard kernel pool-overflow follow-on techniques apply, but the bounded length and the feature-flag state constrain reliability.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED subtract-8 branch (0x1C007649E-0x1C00764DA):
movzx   eax, r14w                   ; options size
sub     eax, 8                      ; size - 8
cmp     ecx, eax
jge     short loc_1C00764BD
lea     r14d, [r9+r8]
jmp     short loc_1C00764C6
loc_1C00764BD:
mov     eax, 0FFF8h
add     r14w, ax                    ; 16-bit size - 8 -> 0FFFEh/0FFFFh for size 6/7
loc_1C00764C6:
movzx   r8d, r14w                   ; Size (underflowed)
lea     rcx, [r15+8]                ; dst = mapped output buffer + 8
call    memmove                     ; OVERSIZED copy

; PATCHED (feature-gated < 8 zero-fill, 0x1C00754A5-0x1C007550B):
lea     rcx, g_Feature_1191968056_61054031_FeatureDescriptorDetails
call    EvaluateCurrentState
mov     ebx, 8
test    eax, eax
jz      short loc_1C0075512         ; feature off -> old subtract-8 path
cmp     si, bx                      ; options size < 8 ?
jb      short loc_1C0075502         ; yes -> zero-fill
sub     si, bx                      ; size - 8 (safe, size >= 8)
loc_1C0075502:
movzx   r8d, si
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, r15
call    memset                      ; SAFE

breakpoints.why: Entry of the accept IRP completion routine. Break here to observe options processing; word [connection+0x18] is options_size and byte [connection+0xAC] selects the copy branch.
The memmove in the subtract-8 branch. Read r8 (the size argument); for options size 6/7 it holds 0xFFFE/0xFFFF.

watch: word [connection+0x18] = options_size (must be 6 or 7 to trigger)
byte [connection+0xAC] (must be 0 to reach the subtract-8 branch)
r14 at 0x1c00764c2 after 'add r14w, ax' = copy length (0xFFFE/0xFFFF on underflow)
r15 = destination (mapped output buffer base used by the copy)
r8 at the memmove call = the copy length
key_offsets: 0x1c0076401: 'cmp dword [rbx+18h], 6' — the only guard on options size
0x1c007645e: 'jz loc_1C007649E' — selects the subtract-8 branch when [conn+0xAC]==0
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Open an AFD socket via WSASocket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM, IPPROTO_TCP) or CreateFileW(L"\\\\.\\Device\\Afd", ...). 2. Bind, listen, and post AcceptEx/WSAAccept. 3. Complete a connection whose options field drives [connection+0x18] to 6 or 7 with [connection+0xAC]==0. 4. The subtract-8 branch computes the wrapped length and the memmove overruns the output buffer.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At 0x1c00764da the memmove size (r8) is 0xFFFE/0xFFFF, and the copy overruns the mapped output buffer into adjacent memory, typically BSOD PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA or BAD_POOL_HEADER.
debugging_aids.notes: The vulnerable path requires the connection byte [connection+0xAC] == 0 (the subtract-8 branch) and options_size of exactly 6 or 7. The overflow length is a 16-bit value, capped at ~64 KB. The feature flag g_Feature_1191968056_61054031 gates the patched fix; with it disabled the patched binary still contains the vulnerable subtract-8 path.

security_findings.entry_point: AFD listen completion — reached via the TDI listen-completion path on \Device\Afd
security_findings.description: AfdTLListenComplete (unpatched 0x1C005AB60, patched 0x1C0059850) runs its abort branch only when the completion status a2 < 0 (a failed listen). In that branch the unpatched code clears state flags ([conn+8] &= ~3, conn[0xA8] and conn[0xAA] written from the IRP, *conn &= ~4) and releases the conn+0x30 spinlock WITHOUT draining the pending listen-IRP list at conn+0x70; AfdDerefTLBaseEndpoint then runs. Any IRPs still queued on conn+0x70 therefore survive across the endpoint dereference and can later reference a freed endpoint. The patched version inserts EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_2949905720_61057653) and, when enabled, calls AfdDrainListeningIrps(conn, &LockHandle, a2) before the lock is released. That helper walks the conn+0x70 list (each list entry lies at IRP+0xA8, so IRP = entry - 0xA8), clears each IRP's cancel routine, completes it with the failing status, and re-acquires the conn+0x30 spinlock.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Create a listening AFD socket and arrange for its listen to complete with a failure status (a2 < 0), selecting the abort branch. 2. Have listen/wait IRPs queued on the endpoint's conn+0x70 list at the time of the failed completion. 3. In the unpatched build the abort branch clears state flags and releases the conn+0x30 spinlock without draining conn+0x70, then AfdDerefTLBaseEndpoint runs. 4. IRPs remaining on conn+0x70 after the endpoint dereference are stale.
security_findings.primitive: Stale IRPs left queued on conn+0x70 across AfdDerefTLBaseEndpoint on the failed-listen path. If the dereference frees the endpoint, later handling of those IRPs references freed memory. The precise exploitability (use-after-free vs. leaked/stuck IRP) is not fully established from the diff.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED (a2 < 0 branch, no drain):
and     dword ptr [rbx+8], 0FFFFFFFCh
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+90h]
mov     [rbx+0A8h], ax
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+78h]
mov     [rbx+0AAh], ax
mov     eax, 0FFFBh
and     [rbx], ax
call    cs:__imp_KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

; PATCHED (drain added before release):
and     dword ptr [rbx+8], 0FFFFFFFCh
lea     rcx, g_Feature_2949905720_61057653_FeatureDescriptorDetails
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+90h]
mov     [rbx+0A8h], ax
movzx   eax, word ptr [rsi+78h]
mov     [rbx+0AAh], ax
mov     eax, 0FFFBh
and     [rbx], ax
call    EvaluateCurrentState
test    eax, eax
je      short loc_1C005995F
mov     r8d, ebp               ; status
lea     rdx, [rsp+88h+LockHandle]
mov     rcx, rbx               ; connection
call    AfdDrainListeningIrps  ; NEW: drain conn+0x70 list
call    cs:__imp_KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock

breakpoints.why: Entry of the listen completion handler. rbx becomes the connection object; the pending listen-IRP list head is conn+0x70. The abort branch runs when the completion status is negative.
The KeReleaseInStackQueuedSpinLock in the abort branch. Check whether [conn+0x70] still points at queued IRPs (i.e. [conn+0x70] != conn+0x70).

watch: [rbx+0x70] = pending listen-IRP list Flink; != rbx+0x70 means undrained IRPs
[rbx+0xA8], [rbx+0xAA] = state values written from the IRP
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Create a listening AFD socket and drive its listen to complete with a failure status. 2. Have listen/wait IRPs queued on conn+0x70 at that moment. 3. The unpatched abort branch releases the conn+0x30 lock without draining conn+0x70, then AfdDerefTLBaseEndpoint runs.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At 0x1c005ac54, [conn+0x70] does not point back to conn+0x70, indicating undrained IRPs; subsequent access to them after the endpoint dereference can touch freed memory.
debugging_aids.notes: The abort branch runs only for a negative completion status. The list head is conn+0x70; each list entry lies at IRP+0xA8, so IRP = entry - 0xA8. The feature flag g_Feature_2949905720_61057653 gates the patched drain. AfdDrainListeningIrps completes each IRP with the failing status, clears its cancel routine, and re-acquires the conn+0x30 spinlock before returning.

security_findings.entry_point: AFD receive completion path — reached via NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd
call_chain: AfdSanFastCompleteRequest (receive completion path with cancel-routine setup)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing state validation under feature-flag-off configuration (hardening)
security_findings.description: The original TOCTOU characterization is NOT supported by the binaries. In AfdSanFastCompleteRequest (unpatched 0x1C0078630, patched 0x1C0077700) the cancel-routine set, the Irp->Cancel check, the connection-teardown-state check, and the list insertion all execute while the conn+0x30 spinlock is held; AfdSanCancelRequest also acquires that lock, so no concurrent cancel can interleave inside this region. The actual change is defensive: the unpatched queue condition is Irp->Cancel(Irp+0x44)==0 && (!EvaluateCurrentState(flag) || conn[0x91]==0), so when the feature flag is disabled the teardown-state byte conn[0x91] is not checked and the receive IRP is queued onto conn+0x70 regardless. The patched condition is Irp->Cancel==0 && conn[0x91]==0, making the teardown-state check unconditional. The cancel routine is AfdSanCancelRequest in both builds. This is a hardening improvement, not a race.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Establish a TCP connection over an AFD socket and issue receive operations that reach AfdSanFastCompleteRequest. 2. Arrange for the connection to be in teardown (conn[0x91] != 0) at completion time. 3. With the feature flag disabled, the unpatched build skips the conn[0x91] check and queues the receive IRP onto conn+0x70 even though the connection is tearing down; the patched build always checks conn[0x91] and completes the IRP as cancelled instead. This is a state-validation gap, not a timing race (all under the conn+0x30 spinlock).
security_findings.primitive: Not a standalone exploit primitive. The change removes a feature-flag-off configuration in which a receive IRP could be queued onto a connection already in teardown. Impact depends on downstream handling of that queued IRP and is not demonstrable from the diff alone.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED (state check gated by feature flag):
lea     rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
xchg    rax, [rsi+68h]              ; Irp->CancelRoutine
cmp     byte ptr [rsi+44h], 0      ; Irp->Cancel
jne     short loc_1C00789D2
lea     rcx, g_Feature_757798200_60363861_FeatureDescriptorDetails
call    EvaluateCurrentState
test    eax, eax
je      short loc_1C0078995        ; flag off -> SKIP [r15+91h] check, queue IRP
cmp     byte ptr [r15+91h], 0
jne     short loc_1C00789D2
; queue IRP onto conn+0x70 list (entry at rsi+0xA8, head at r15+0x70)

; PATCHED (state check unconditional, feature call removed):
lea     rax, AfdSanCancelRequest
xchg    rax, [rsi+68h]
cmp     byte ptr [rsi+44h], 0
jne     short loc_1C0077A92
cmp     byte ptr [r15+91h], 0      ; always checked
jne     short loc_1C0077A92
; queue IRP onto conn+0x70 list

breakpoints.why: Entry of the receive completion path. rsi = IRP, r15 = connection object. The pending-IRP list head is conn+0x70; the whole cancel/queue sequence runs under the conn+0x30 spinlock.

breakpoints.symbol: afd_unpatched EvaluateCurrentState call in the queue decision
breakpoints.why: The feature-flag call in the unpatched queue decision. If it returns 0, the [r15+0x91] teardown check is skipped and the IRP is queued unconditionally.

watch: rsi+0xA8 / rsi+0xB0 (IRP list-entry fields queued onto the list)
key_offsets: 0x1c0078971: 'xchg [rsi+68h], rax' — sets the cancel routine (under the lock)
0x1c0078982: 'call EvaluateCurrentState' — feature gate; when off, [r15+0x91] is not checked
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Establish a TCP connection and issue receive operations reaching AfdSanFastCompleteRequest. 2. Put the connection into teardown (conn[0x91] != 0). 3. With the feature flag disabled, the unpatched build queues the receive IRP onto conn+0x70 without checking conn[0x91]; the patched build completes it as cancelled.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the unpatched build with the flag off, the IRP list entry (rsi+0xA8) is inserted onto conn+0x70 even though conn[0x91] is set. This is a state-validation gap; the whole sequence runs under the conn+0x30 spinlock, so no concurrent cancel interleaves.
debugging_aids.notes: The cancel routine is AfdSanCancelRequest in both builds. The feature flag g_Feature_757798200_60363861 gated whether conn[0x91] was checked in the unpatched build; the patched build removes the gate so the check is always enforced. Severity is low: this is a hardening change, not a race, and no concrete exploit primitive is demonstrable from the diff.

summary: The patch changes three functions in the Windows AFD (Ancillary Function Driver) for Winsock in a security-relevant way. The primary fix is in AfdSanAcceptCore (unpatched 0x1C007608C, patched 0x1C007508C): in the options-copy block (guarded only by options_size >= 6), the branch taken when the connection byte at [connection+0xAC] == 0 computes the copy length as options_size - 8 in a 16-bit register. Options sizes of 6 or 7 pass the guard and wrap to 0xFFFE/0xFFFF, driving an oversized memmove (up to ~64 KB) into the MDL-mapped output buffer; the patched build adds a feature-gated 'options_size < 8 -> memset' path. The other branch subtracts 2 and cannot underflow given the guard, and is unchanged. The second change (AfdTLListenComplete, unpatched 0x1C005AB60, patched 0x1C0059850) adds a feature-gated AfdDrainListeningIrps call on the failed-listen (status < 0) branch to drain and complete pending listen IRPs at conn+0x70 before releasing the conn+0x30 spinlock and dereferencing the endpoint. The third change (AfdSanFastCompleteRequest, unpatched 0x1C0078630, patched 0x1C0077700) makes the connection-teardown-state check (conn[0x91]) unconditional, where it was previously skipped when a feature flag was off; this is a hardening change, not a TOCTOU race (the whole sequence runs under the conn+0x30 spinlock), and is downgraded to low severity. All fixes are gated by (or, for the third, remove) EvaluateCurrentState feature flags, so with a flag disabled the patched binary may still contain the pre-fix behavior for findings 1 and 2.
